words.definitionEn: what you do, say or think as a result of something that has happened
words.exampleEn: to provoke/cause/get a reaction

words.definitionEn: the true situation and the problems that actually exist in life, in contrast to how you would like life to be
words.exampleEn: She refuses to face reality.

words.definitionEn: a piece of paper or an electronic document that shows that goods or services have been paid for
words.exampleEn: Can I have a receipt, please?

words.definitionEn: an official suggestion about the best thing to do
words.exampleEn: to accept/reject a recommendation

words.definitionEn: to cook food in an oven without extra fat or liquid; to be cooked in this way
words.exampleEn: bake (something), to bake bread/biscuits/cookies

words.definitionEn: a thing you say or write that mentions somebody/something else; the act of mentioning somebody/something
words.exampleEn: reference to somebody/something, She made no reference to her illness but only to her future plans.

words.definitionEn: a situation in which different things exist in equal, correct or good amounts
words.exampleEn: This newspaper maintains a good balance in its presentation of different opinions.

words.definitionEn: to show the image of somebody/something on the surface of something such as a mirror, water or glass
words.exampleEn: be reflected (in something), His face was reflected in the mirror.

words.definitionEn: to put your body or something else into a position where it is steady and does not fall
words.exampleEn: balance on something, How long can you balance on one leg?

words.definitionEn: at regular intervals or times
words.exampleEn: We meet regularly to discuss the progress of the project.

words.definitionEn: to refuse to accept or consider something
words.exampleEn: to reject an argument/a hypothesis/a notion/a plan

words.definitionEn: show or make a connection between two or more things
words.exampleEn: relate something, I found it difficult to relate the two ideas in my mind.

words.definitionEn: connected with something/somebody in some way
words.exampleEn: related to something/somebody, The amount of protein you need is directly related to your lifestyle.

words.definitionEn: the way in which two people, groups or countries behave towards each other or deal with each other
words.exampleEn: diplomatic/international/foreign relations

words.definitionEn: considered and judged by being compared with something else
words.exampleEn: You must consider the relative merits of the two plans.

words.definitionEn: a person who is in the same family as somebody else
words.exampleEn: a close/distant relative

words.definitionEn: calm and not anxious or worried
words.exampleEn: He appeared relaxed and confident before the match.

words.definitionEn: helping you to rest and become less anxious
words.exampleEn: a relaxing evening with friends

words.definitionEn: the act of setting a person or an animal free; the state of being set free
words.exampleEn: release of somebody/something, The government has been working to secure the release of the hostages.

words.definitionEn: to let somebody come out of a place where they have been kept or stuck and unable to leave or move
words.exampleEn: release somebody, to release a prisoner

words.definitionEn: that can be trusted to do something well; that you can rely on
words.exampleEn: We are looking for someone who is reliable and hard-working.

words.definitionEn: the belief in the existence of a god or gods, and the activities that are connected with the worship of them, or in the teachings of a spiritual leader
words.exampleEn: Is there always a conflict between science and religion?

words.definitionEn: connected with religion or with a particular religion
words.exampleEn: religious beliefs/convictions/faith

words.definitionEn: to continue to be something; to be still in the same state or condition
words.exampleEn: remain + adj., to remain silent

words.definitionEn: to help somebody remember something, especially something important that they must do
words.exampleEn: remind somebody/yourself, I'm sorry, I've forgotten your name. Can you remind me?

words.definitionEn: far away from places where other people live
words.exampleEn: a remote village/island/location/region

words.definitionEn: an official rule that says that something is not allowed
words.exampleEn: a smoking ban

words.definitionEn: an amount of money that you regularly pay so that you can use a house, room, etc.
words.exampleEn: I earn just about enough to pay the rent.

words.definitionEn: to regularly pay money to somebody so that you can use something that they own, such as a house, a room, some land, etc.
words.exampleEn: Are you looking to buy or rent?

words.definitionEn: an act of repairing something
words.exampleEn: They agreed to pay the costs of any repairs.

words.definitionEn: an event that is very similar to something that happened before
words.exampleEn: repeat of something, She didn't want a repeat performance of what had happened the night before.

words.definitionEn: to decide or say officially that something is not allowed
words.exampleEn: ban something, The law effectively bans smoking in all public places.

words.definitionEn: happening, said or done many times
words.exampleEn: repeated absences from work

words.definitionEn: to be a member of a group of people and act or speak for them at an event, a meeting, etc.
words.exampleEn: The competition attracted over 500 contestants representing eight different countries.

words.definitionEn: to ask for something or ask somebody to do something in a polite or formal way
words.exampleEn: request something, She requested permission to film at the White House.

words.definitionEn: to need something; to depend on somebody/something
words.exampleEn: require something, These pets require a lot of care and attention.

words.definitionEn: an arrangement for a seat on a plane or train, a room in a hotel, etc. to be kept for you
words.exampleEn: I'll call the restaurant and make a reservation.

words.definitionEn: a supply of something that a country, an organization or a person has and can use, especially to increase their wealth
words.exampleEn: the exploitation of minerals and other natural resources

words.definitionEn: a strong feeling of approval of somebody/something because of their good qualities or achievements
words.exampleEn: I have the utmost respect for her and her work.

words.definitionEn: to have a very good opinion of somebody/something; to admire somebody/something
words.exampleEn: I respect Jack's opinion on most subjects.

words.definitionEn: a duty to deal with or take care of somebody/something, so that you may be blamed if something goes wrong
words.exampleEn: to be in a position of responsibility

words.definitionEn: having the job or duty of doing something or taking care of somebody/something, so that you may be blamed if something goes wrong
words.exampleEn: responsible for doing something, Mike is responsible for designing the entire project.

words.definitionEn: to make something happen
words.exampleEn: A heavy frost could result in loss of the crop.

words.definitionEn: to leave your job and stop working, especially because you have reached a particular age or because you are ill; to tell somebody they must stop doing their job
words.exampleEn: He is retiring next year after 30 years with the company.

words.definitionEn: having retired from work
words.exampleEn: a retired doctor/teacher/officer/general

words.definitionEn: to change your opinions or plans, for example because of something you have learned
words.exampleEn: I can see I will have to revise my opinions of his abilities now.

words.definitionEn: an increase in an amount, a number or a level
words.exampleEn: The industry is feeling the effects of recent price rises.

words.definitionEn: the possibility of something bad happening at some time in the future; a situation that could be dangerous or have a bad result
words.exampleEn: The health risks are very low.

words.definitionEn: to put something valuable or important in a dangerous situation, in which it could be lost or damaged
words.exampleEn: He risked his life to save her.

words.definitionEn: a machine that can perform a complicated series of tasks by itself
words.exampleEn: These cars are built by robots.

words.definitionEn: the lowest part of something, especially the part or surface on which it rests or stands
words.exampleEn: The lamp has a heavy base.

words.definitionEn: a small loaf of bread for one person
words.exampleEn: Soup and a roll: £3.50

words.definitionEn: to turn over and over and move in a particular direction; to make a round object do this
words.exampleEn: + adv./prep., The ball rolled down the hill.

words.definitionEn: connected with or about love or a sexual relationship
words.exampleEn: a romantic candlelit dinner

words.definitionEn: to use a particular city, town, etc. as the main place for a business, holiday, etc.
words.exampleEn: be based in… , The organization is now based in Geneva.

words.definitionEn: very strong thick string made by twisting thinner strings, wires, etc. together
words.exampleEn: The rope broke and she fell 50 metres onto the rocks.

words.definitionEn: having a surface that is not even or regular
words.exampleEn: rough ground

words.definitionEn: connected with or belonging to the king or queen of a country
words.exampleEn: the royal family

words.definitionEn: a game played by two teams of 13 or 15 players, using an oval ball which may be kicked or carried. Teams try to put the ball over the other team’s line.
words.exampleEn: to play a game of rugby

words.definitionEn: to control and have authority over a country, a group of people, etc.
words.exampleEn: rule (something), At that time, King John ruled England.

words.definitionEn: forming the part of something that is most necessary and from which other things develop
words.exampleEn: basic information/facts/ideas

words.definitionEn: the state of being safe and protected from danger or harm
words.exampleEn: in safety, a place where children can play in safety

words.definitionEn: a sheet of strong cloth which the wind blows against to make a boat or ship travel through the water
words.exampleEn: As the boat moved down the river the wind began to fill the sails.

words.definitionEn: a person who works on a ship as a member of the crew
words.exampleEn: a crew of two officers and 13 sailors

words.definitionEn: a number of people or things taken from a larger group and used in tests to provide information about the group
words.exampleEn: The interviews were given to a random sample of students.

words.definitionEn: the way things are organized or arranged; how often something happens
words.exampleEn: We are in contact on a regular basis.

words.definitionEn: a substance that consists of very small fine grains of rock. Sand is found on beaches, in deserts, etc.
words.exampleEn: a grain of sand

words.definitionEn: to look quickly but not very carefully at a document, etc.
words.exampleEn: scan something for something, I scanned the list quickly for my name.

words.definitionEn: involving science; connected with science
words.exampleEn: a scientific discovery/theory/fact

words.definitionEn: a written text of a play, film, broadcast, talk, etc.
words.exampleEn: That line isn't in the original script.

words.definitionEn: a work of art that is a solid figure or object made by carving or shaping wood, stone, clay, metal, etc.
words.exampleEn: a marble sculpture of Venus

words.definitionEn: connected with teaching children of 11–18 years
words.exampleEn: secondary teachers

words.definitionEn: the activities involved in protecting a country, building or person against attack, danger, etc.
words.exampleEn: national/homeland security (= the defence of a country)

words.definitionEn: the small hard part produced by a plant, from which a new plant can grow
words.exampleEn: a packet of wild flower seeds

words.definitionEn: a device that is placed inside a car engine, clock, radio, etc. and that produces the electricity that makes it work
words.exampleEn: to replace the batteries

words.definitionEn: a fight between armies, ships or planes, especially during a war; a violent fight between groups of people
words.exampleEn: Napoleon was defeated at the Battle of Waterloo.

words.definitionEn: able to make good judgements based on reason and experience rather than emotion; practical
words.exampleEn: She's a sensible sort of person.

words.definitionEn: to divide into different parts or groups; to divide things into different parts or groups
words.exampleEn: Stir the sauce constantly so that it does not separate.

words.definitionEn: in a serious way
words.exampleEn: to be seriously ill/injured/wounded/hurt

words.definitionEn: a person who works in another person’s house, and cooks, cleans, etc. for them
words.exampleEn: a domestic servant

words.definitionEn: a group of similar things that belong together in some way
words.exampleEn: a set of six chairs

words.definitionEn: to prepare or arrange something so that it is ready for use or in position
words.exampleEn: set something, Have you set the alarm clock?

words.definitionEn: an environment where something is located; the place at which something happens
words.exampleEn: a rural/an ideal/a beautiful/an idyllic setting

words.definitionEn: the state of being male or female
words.exampleEn: How can you tell what sex a fish is?

words.definitionEn: connected with the physical activity of sex
words.exampleEn: sexual behaviour/activity/desire

words.definitionEn: an act of shaking somebody/something
words.exampleEn: She gave him a shake to wake him.

words.definitionEn: one part of something that is divided between two or more people
words.exampleEn: share of something, Next year we hope to have a greater share of the market.

words.definitionEn: having a fine edge or point, especially of something that can cut or make a hole in something
words.exampleEn: a sharp knife

words.definitionEn: a flat board, made of wood, metal, glass, etc., fixed to the wall or forming part of a cupboard, bookcase, etc., for things to be placed on
words.exampleEn: I helped him put up some shelves in his bedroom.

words.definitionEn: the hard outer part of eggs, nuts, some seeds, and some animals
words.exampleEn: We collected shells on the beach.

words.definitionEn: a period of time worked by a group of workers who start work as another group finishes
words.exampleEn: to work a shift

words.definitionEn: to produce or reflect light; to be bright
words.exampleEn: The sun shone brightly in a cloudless sky.

words.definitionEn: smooth and bright; reflecting the light
words.exampleEn: shiny black hair

words.definitionEn: to fire a gun or other weapon; to fire something from a weapon
words.exampleEn: Don't shoot—I surrender.

words.definitionEn: nervous or embarrassed about meeting and speaking to other people
words.exampleEn: He is not exactly the shy and retiring type.

words.definitionEn: the ability to see
words.exampleEn: to lose your sight (= to become blind)

words.definitionEn: a movement or sound that you make to give somebody information, instructions, a warning, etc.
words.exampleEn: a danger/warning/distress signal

words.definitionEn: to make a movement or sound to give somebody a message, an order, etc.
words.exampleEn: Don't fire until I signal.

words.definitionEn: where there is little or no sound; making little or no sound
words.exampleEn: At last the traffic fell silent.

words.definitionEn: showing a lack of thought, understanding, or judgement
words.exampleEn: a silly idea/question/name

words.definitionEn: the state of being similar to somebody/something but not exactly the same
words.exampleEn: similarity between A and B, The report highlights the similarity between the two groups.

words.definitionEn: in almost the same way
words.exampleEn: It is a little cheaper than other similarly sized cars.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize how easy or basic something is
words.exampleEn: To order, simply click here.

words.definitionEn: from a time in the past until a later past time, or until now
words.exampleEn: She went for a run on Monday and has not been seen since.

words.definitionEn: to go down below the surface or towards the bottom of a liquid or soft substance
words.exampleEn: The ship sank to the bottom of the sea.

words.definitionEn: the quality of giving pleasure to the senses or to the mind
words.exampleEn: beauty of something, the beauty of the sunset/of poetry/of his singing

words.definitionEn: a thin flat piece of bread, meat, cheese, etc. that has been cut off a larger piece; a piece of cake that has been cut from a larger cake
words.exampleEn: Cut the meat into thin slices.

words.definitionEn: to cut something into slices
words.exampleEn: to slice (up) onions

words.definitionEn: a little
words.exampleEn: a slightly different version

words.definitionEn: to go or to make something/somebody go at a slower speed or be less active
words.exampleEn: Economic growth has slowed a little.

words.definitionEn: looking clean and neat; well dressed in fashionable and/or formal clothes
words.exampleEn: I have to be smart for work.

words.definitionEn: completely flat and even, without any rough areas or holes
words.exampleEn: the smooth surface of the metal

words.definitionEn: a black and yellow flying insect that can sting (= touch your skin and make you feel a sharp pain).Bees live in large groups and make honey (= a sweet sticky substance that is good to eat).
words.exampleEn: Bees were buzzing in the clover.

words.definitionEn: the programs used by a computer for doing particular jobs
words.exampleEn: management/editing software

words.definitionEn: the top layer of the earth in which plants, trees, etc. grow
words.exampleEn: instruments for measuring soil moisture

words.definitionEn: hard; not in the form of a liquid or gas
words.exampleEn: The planet Jupiter may have no solid surface at all.

words.definitionEn: a substance or an object that is solid, not a liquid or a gas
words.exampleEn: liquids and solids

words.definitionEn: to arrange things in groups or in a particular order according to their type, etc.; to separate things of one type from others
words.exampleEn: sort something, I started at the bottom, answering phones and sorting the mail.

words.definitionEn: located in the south or facing south; connected with or typical of the south part of the world or a region
words.exampleEn: the southern slopes of the mountains

words.definitionEn: connected with or intended for one particular thing only
words.exampleEn: liquid vitamins specifically designed for children

words.definitionEn: the amount of money that is spent by a government, an organization or a person
words.exampleEn: to increase/cut/reduce spending

words.definitionEn: having a strong taste because spices have been added to it
words.exampleEn: I don't like spicy food.

words.definitionEn: the part of a person that includes their mind, feelings and character rather than their body
words.exampleEn: You are underestimating the power of the human spirit to overcome difficulties.

words.definitionEn: involving speaking rather than writing; expressed in speech rather than in writing
words.exampleEn: spoken English

words.definitionEn: a small round area that has a different colour or feels different from the surface it is on
words.exampleEn: Which has spots, the leopard or the tiger?

words.definitionEn: to affect or make something affect, be known by, or be used by more and more people
words.exampleEn: The news had spread and was causing great excitement.

words.definitionEn: a large sports ground surrounded by rows of seats and usually other buildings
words.exampleEn: a football/sports stadium

words.definitionEn: all the workers employed in an organization considered as a group
words.exampleEn: medical/nursing/teaching/coaching staff

words.definitionEn: average or normal rather than having special or unusual features
words.exampleEn: the standard rate of tax (= paid by everyone)

words.definitionEn: a level of quality, especially one that people think is acceptable
words.exampleEn: There has been a fall in living standards.

words.definitionEn: a strong feeling that something/somebody exists or is true; confidence that something/somebody is good or right
words.exampleEn: belief in something/somebody, I admire his passionate belief in what he is doing.

words.definitionEn: provided or controlled by the government of a country
words.exampleEn: state officials/agencies

words.definitionEn: to formally write or say something, especially in a careful and clear way
words.exampleEn: state something, He has already stated his intention to run for election.

words.definitionEn: a collection of information shown in numbers
words.exampleEn: Analysis of crime statistics showed high levels of violent crime within the area.
